repo_prior_art.cite: none; the closest active stat entries concern PN/PS weak-event inference or COT efficiency, not an exogenous illness-death initiation rule

published_prior_art.cite: MartinussenKnudsenRytgaard2026Debiased — fixed-window illness-death EIF for its natural-hazard-normalized intervention

repo_prior_art.cite: stat_pn_weak_event_honest_inference — Poisson/Gaussian effective-count frontier for PN/PS, not a CAR illness-death marked initiation process

published_prior_art.cite: SchindlShenKennedy2024IncrementalContinuous — effective-sample-size lower and Gaussian-limit theory for exponentially tilted static continuous exposures; AiHuangZhang2026UniformContinuousTreatment — Gaussian uniform bands for ordinary continuous treatment

repo_prior_art.cite: stat_pn_weak_event_honest_inference — bounded-count honest-diameter obstruction, though for a distinct PN/PS ratio target

published_prior_art.cite: ArmstrongKolesar2020HonestCI — bias-aware Hölder interval geometry, without finite-count CAR multistate calibration

soundness_flags.label: def:one-step, def:bias-aware-interval, and oeq:honest-diameter
soundness_flags.one_line: The fitted hazards in \(\widehat R_{b_n}\) are not a specified measurable learning procedure or a quantified learner class, so \(C^{\mathrm{BA}}_{n,\alpha}[b_n]\), \(\mathcal B^G_\alpha\), and its claimed honesty are not yet determinate objects.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:observed-hazard-validity and lem:extension-invariance
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The correspondence between the full-filtration deterministic-initiation intervention and observed transition hazards is assumed, not identified from the observed law.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The exact-time causal interpretation and extension-invariance claim are conditional on every compatible full-data extension satisfying ass:consistency, ass:observed-hazard-validity, and ass:car.

source_verification_receipts.locator: pp. 4–5, definition of \(\tilde g\) and §3.1 Theorem 3.1; p. 7, §4 Theorem 4.1
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper’s window intervention normalizes a density built from the natural initiation hazard, derives its illness-death EIF, and then gives a covariate extension; it does not state a shrinking-window marked-Poisson limit.

source_verification_receipts.locator: p. 9, Theorem 2; pp. 12–13, Theorem 4 discussion; pp. 47–52, proof of Theorem 4
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper proves an \(n/\delta\) effective-sample-size lower bound and a scaled Gaussian one-step limit under Lindeberg conditions for static exponentially tilted continuous exposures.
The paper uses weighted local-linear estimation, proves uniform Bahadur representations, and establishes bootstrap uniform confidence bands for general continuous-treatment response functions.
The paper develops bias-aware honest intervals for kernel/local-polynomial nonparametric regression, including efficiency and bandwidth comparisons.

source_verification_receipts.version: Journal of Business & Economic Statistics 44(1) (2026), pp. 67–79
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper proposes kernel-localized doubly robust continuous-treatment DML with cross-fitting and asymptotic-normality conditions for nuisance learning.

source_verification_receipts.version: author preprint corresponding to JASA 119 (2024), pp. 1541–1553
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper establishes cross-fitted, doubly robust survival-curve asymptotic linearity and Gaussian inference under nuisance-rate and positivity conditions, not shrinking initiation windows.
The paper studies minimax CATE estimation in Hölder-smooth nonparametric models and constructs a rate-optimal local-polynomial estimator.

source_verification_receipts.version: Journal of the Royal Statistical Society: Series B 79(4) (2017), pp. 1229–1245
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper introduces a kernel-smoothed doubly robust continuous-treatment effect method with mild smoothness assumptions and data-driven bandwidth selection.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the generic instruction to fit hazards with a named learner family and foldwise clipping/positivity rules, or redefine \(\mathcal B^G_\alpha\) over explicit learner-bandwidth pairs with uniform remainder assumptions.
Turn the critical-law handle into a stated first coverage result: specify a Poisson-calibrated interval and formulate its conditional coverage target, including the no-local-initiation atom.

Add a concrete two-point or Poisson-experiment lower-bound route for the honest-diameter question, so the eventual rate/diameter frontier is comparable with the active PN/PS bounded-count result rather than only adjacent to it.

recommended_next_step: Revise the unspecified cross-fitted learner into a determinate procedure or procedure class, then resubmit this otherwise field-grade rare-initiation phase-transition angle.
